title: Sporobolus aculeatus
taxonomy: Sporobolus aculeatus is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Sporobolus, which consists of various species of grasses, mainly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The family Poaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as grasses, with over 12,000 species worldwide. Sporobolus aculeatus is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Africa, Asia, and Australia. This species of grass is often found growing in wetland areas and along rivers.

intro: Sporobolus aculeatus, also known as sand dropseed, is a type of perennial grass native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
